acceptance_targets: [CH10_AWARD_01] By the end of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:233, at least one complication introduced at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:97-123 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:175-219 has changed what a character will do next. A cold reader should be able to name that new plan, cost, or consequence in one sentence; if the ending still only confirms that Draco and Hermione are gentler with each other, this finding fails.

[CH10_CRAFT_01] In revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:65-71, at least one domestic beat that is currently summary unfolds through present action or dialogue and leaves a visible behavior change by the end of that beat. If this span remains only an inventory of routines and observations, this finding fails.

[CH10_CRAFT_02] In revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:73 and/or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:199-203, a cold reader can state what Hermione physically does during an examination, what immediate observable change occurs in Draco's body or Mark during contact, and what remains hypothesis rather than fact. If the contact is still summarized only as the Mark going quiet or settling, this finding fails.

[CH10_DIALOGUE_01] Revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:167-177 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:207-219 must still convey systemic bias and Theo noticing Draco's change, but neither exchange may hinge on a single polished diagnosis sentence. At least one speaker in each exchange should restart, hedge, redirect, interrupt, or leave part of the point unsaid in a way that remains character-true.
[R-08] Ch 10's kitchen opening does not re-inventory the stone floor, counters, or single-occupant atmosphere. The scene enters through the kettle failure and the concealment motive.
[length_chapter_10_min_words] Chapter word count is >= 5500 while preserving heading contract.

findings.acceptance_test: By the end of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:233, at least one complication introduced at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:97-123 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:175-219 has changed what a character will do next. A cold reader should be able to name that new plan, cost, or consequence in one sentence; if the ending still only confirms that Draco and Hermione are gentler with each other, this finding fails.
findings.problem: The chapter's late complications are raised and then neutralized, so the major movement reads as confirmation of a state change the reader already understands rather than a chapter-sized turn. Theo brings public gossip, Hermione offers institutional help, Theo names Draco's shift, and the chapter still closes on the same domestic equilibrium with no new plan, cost, or irreversible relational or public consequence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Use the Theo sequence at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:97-123 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:175-233 to make one of the introduced pressures land. Options include forcing Draco to respond to the gossip, having Theo accept or reject Hermione's help in a way that changes future behavior, or letting the Mark conversation alter how the examinations will proceed. The closing beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:223-233 should end on a changed situation, not only a reaffirmed mood.

findings.acceptance_test: In revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:65-71, at least one domestic beat that is currently summary unfolds through present action or dialogue and leaves a visible behavior change by the end of that beat. If this span remains only an inventory of routines and observations, this finding fails.
findings.problem: The chapter's central domestic shift is mostly delivered as montage inventory. Shared meals, books left on his chair, the open library door, loosening formality, and Hermione's laugh are asserted in sequence, but only one of those beats is actually dramatized. In a chapter whose main job is to render tenderness, this middle movement reads like proof-of-progress rather than lived intimacy.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:65-71 so at least one currently summarized domestic beat becomes a full on-page exchange. The best targets are the first habitual dinner, the books-left-on-his-chair beat, or the Septimus joke/laugh at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:71. Keep the time passage, but let the reader inhabit one quiet routine deeply enough to feel the register change instead of being told it happened.

findings.acceptance_test: In revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:73 and/or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:199-203, a cold reader can state what Hermione physically does during an examination, what immediate observable change occurs in Draco's body or Mark during contact, and what remains hypothesis rather than fact. If the contact is still summarized only as the Mark going quiet or settling, this finding fails.
findings.problem: When the chapter reaches the most bodily consequence of their new tenderness, it retreats into abstract summary and Draco's refusal to look. 'The Mark went quiet under her hands' and later 'it settles' are exactly the kind of physically consequential contact this novel's premise asks the chapter to render, but the prose stops at statement level. That is sanitized or timid handling of the Mark examinations rather than scene presence.
findings.rewrite_direction: Expand snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:73 and/or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:199-203 into a concrete examination beat. Show what Hermione physically does, what Draco's arm and body do in response, what relief or destabilization is observable, and where uncertainty begins. Draco can still evade the implication, but the revision needs bodily data before the evasion. If useful, borrow space from the surrounding summary at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:65-73 or the post-Theo return at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:223-225.

findings.acceptance_test: Revised snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:167-177 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:207-219 must still convey systemic bias and Theo noticing Draco's change, but neither exchange may hinge on a single polished diagnosis sentence. At least one speaker in each exchange should restart, hedge, redirect, interrupt, or leave part of the point unsaid in a way that remains character-true.
findings.problem: The chapter's sharpest conversations clean themselves into thesis statements and diagnosis. Hermione's licensing analysis, Theo's prejudice summary, and Theo's final read of Draco all land as neat mini-speeches instead of audience-calibrated, partial speech. That pushes Theo toward the 'best friend with the perfect observation' failure the style bible forbids and smooths Hermione's pressure-speech into policy prose.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen the exchanges at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:167-177 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:207-219 without losing the content. Let Hermione arrive at the point through correction, specificity, or interruption rather than a clean thesis sentence, and let Theo's final honesty come sideways, with a dodge, restart, or undercut that suits his calibrated voice.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 10's kitchen opening does not re-inventory the stone floor, counters, or single-occupant atmosphere. The scene enters through the kettle failure and the concealment motive.
findings.evidence: Ch 2 lines 156-157: kitchen as solitary space ('one mug on the rack… lone plate drying beside it'); Ch 10 lines 1956-1962: 'The kitchen was the oldest part of the Manor that still worked like a kitchen: stone floor, scarred counters, a kettle that had sat on the same stretch of counter since before his grandfather's time'; Ch 10 line 1958: 'Better to manage a manual flame than explain to Granger that his household magic was failing along with everyone else's'
findings.problem: Draco's isolation in the Manor kitchen is established in Ch 2 (single mug, lone plate, eating standing because choosing one chair means seeing the other three empty). Ch 10 (line 1956-1962) re-proves the same baseline — solitary kitchen use, failing domestic magic — without materially new leverage or consequence. The failing kettle is a new symptom, but the emotional note (one person in a space meant for more) duplicates Ch 2.
findings.rewrite_direction: Enter the Ch 10 kitchen scene through the new element (the enchanted kettle failing, his choice to hide the failure from Granger) rather than re-describing the kitchen's age, surfaces, and solitary atmosphere. The reader knows this kitchen; show what has changed.

findings.acceptance_test: Chapter word count is >= 5500 while preserving heading contract.
findings.rewrite_direction: Add causally necessary scene material that strengthens the chapter engine, increases pressure, and lands the planned state shift without filler.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel is dark, intense, and emotionally devastating. The premise asks for a love story built inside trauma, in a house that may be manipulating its inhabitants, between two people marked by the same war in different ways. The creative risk is not in the darkness — it is in the tenderness. The hardest thing this novel must do is make the quiet moments as fully rendered as the painful ones, and make the desire as specific as the grief. Do not retreat from the body: the Mark examinations are physical events with physical consequences. The kiss is a physical event. The scar is a physical object on a real arm. Do not retreat from the doubt: the Manor's possible manipulation is not a problem to be solved in act three but a condition to be lived inside for the entire novel. Do not retreat from the ugliness of the drawing room scene or the specificity of what Bellatrix did. And do not retreat from the gentleness of the domestic scenes — tenderness between damaged people is harder to write than pain and should be given equal craft attention.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism is a craft failure equivalent to bad prose. When the scene calls for specificity — in pain, in desire, in shame, in violence, in grief — render it. The drawing room scene must feel like being in the room. The Mark examinations must feel like touch. The scar reveal must feel like exposure. Fading to implication at the moment of consequence wastes every scene that built to that moment.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Narcissa. Uncontracted forms reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or Narcissa's default register. An unjustified uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be flagged and revised.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or domestic ones. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters are allowed for compression; three-scene chapters should not all follow the same internal rhythm. Vary dialogue density — some chapters are conversation-heavy, others are predominantly interior. Never give two consecutive chapters the same structural shape. A chapter that builds to a confrontation should not be followed by another chapter that builds to a confrontation; a quiet aftermath chapter should not be followed by another quiet aftermath chapter. The rhythm of the book as a whole should feel like breathing — expansion and contraction, tension and release, pressure and air.
prose_style_profile.diction: Literary but not ornate. The prose should feel crafted without feeling written — precise where precision matters, rough where roughness is true. Avoid both the polished-prestige-novel voice and the genre-fiction utility voice. The diction should sound like someone telling a story who is very good at telling stories but not performing the act of telling.
forbidden_drift_patterns: The metaphor chain — a comparison that extends across three or more sentences and becomes the scene's organizing image rather than a local effect
Emotional stage directions — 'she felt a surge of' or 'something tightened in his chest' or 'a cold knot formed' as substitutes for specific rendered emotion
The retrospective frame — 'she would later realize' or 'he didn't know then' or other future-looking narrative commentary that drains present-tense urgency
Symmetrical sentence pairs — 'She was X. He was Y.' as a repeated structural pattern, especially in dual-POV transitions
The ambient-magic-as-mood fallback — using the Manor's temperature or the Mark's ache to externalize emotional states that should be rendered through behavior and thought
Catalogue of sensory impressions used as scene-setting rather than scene-building — lists of what a room looks, smells, sounds like that delay rather than create dramatic pressure
The literary-fiction semicolon habit — using semicolons to connect observations that would land harder as separate sentences or would reveal more if left unconnected
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, alternating by chapter. Hermione's chapters are in her interiority — analytical, self-aware, faster. Draco's chapters are in his — more guarded, more sensory, the observations sharper and the self-knowledge harder-won. The POV character's biases should be legible without being announced.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable. Default to medium-length sentences with occasional long, accumulating ones and occasional short declaratives for force. Avoid sustained staccato. Avoid sustained flowing prose. The rhythm should shift with scene pressure — tighter under tension, more expansive in quiet and reflective passages.
sensory_bias: tactile — the Mark examinations, the Manor's surfaces, temperature, skin contact, the physical experience of proximity
architectural and spatial — the Manor as sensory environment, corridors, rooms, light, the way space itself communicates
proprioceptive — body awareness, the resonance between them as a felt physical phenomenon, pain and pleasure as data
